Alright, so we are on to Ops Manager, your Operations Manager. So this person will start off as an administrative assistant to the owner typically. So a couple important things to remember when you're hiring an Ops Manager or bringing on an Ops Manager. One, it's important that they see themselves growing with the business because that is going to be something that will typically be required of them. So two, when you are interviewing or hiring this position, make sure that they can go from like 20 to 40 hours because as you scale, you're going to need to make that transition to pull away the additional duties from the owner. So don't hire somebody that is just looking for a part-time job or you're going to have to start over again. And if you are using this system to scale, it's not going to be a matter of years, it will be a matter of months. So keep that in mind. Now they are doing a lot of the administrative stuff. This is probably one of the most important positions that you're going to have at your gym because the operations of the gym is essentially all the day-to-day stuff with key players that are doing specific roles. But the person that is overseeing the whole machine working and has their hand in every little piece is vital to the efficiency and the effectiveness of what you're doing. So if you have a really great salesperson, that's amazing, but they alone aren't going to move the needle and make the business run really well in the customer's eyes. They may just be really good at acquiring new clients, but what happens if you can't retain them? What happens if your fulfillment is not very good? What if the contracts or paperwork aren't getting processed correctly? What if people are trying to put their account down hold and nobody replies back to their email? Those are all the things that the ops manager oversees, so people really think you're running a tight ship. And that's why it's such an important role because at first the owner will do all of that stuff, but a lot of entrepreneurs and gym owners are not very good at that. So you want to hire somebody that complements your weaknesses, which would be a very good analytical organized operator, which is this position. So extremely important, hire the other positions will be really good at what they do, but this is the person that oversees it all. So in my mind, the ops manager is the person that gives you your freedom as an owner, and it's really, really important that you get the right person. So they'll start off, like I said, as a part-time 20 hours a week admin, and they'll really get to embrace your culture and you'll really get to know if they're the right person to take on the 40 hours, 50 hours a week plus eventual bonuses and things like that, that come with becoming a full-time operations manager. And when that happens, at that point, you'll only be as an owner selling, you'll just be selling. And so really, they're running the gym. I mean, because you're focusing on sales and scaling, right? Sales and lead gen and scaling. And so even after that point, once you get to a higher class like E or F, the owner could decide, hey, I don't want to sell anymore either, and you hire some sales managers, one or two. And at that point, they're just coming in and selling. They just, they're coming in, they're selling, they're closing, and then they're leaving. So the ops manager really would be running everything. But it starts off with finding the right person that is willing to do all of that with you at a lower level. So to train this person, there's a very robust click funnels, share funnel training portal that is in this section that you can click and download. And again, replace all of the videos with your own. So then you have a full blown operations manager training portal for your gym specific for you. And we tell you how to replace all those and everything, but make it your own. Also, there's the four hours document. And then in the owners portal, it's how to hire, how to find this person, how to acquire them, what adds to place, what to look for, how to interview them, all of that stuff is in the owners training course. So this sheet, again, is downloadable. If you want to know all of the specific duties that they will be covering, but we went over most of them, and you can share this with them and start building your staff and finding the right ops manager.
